title: DOM Manipulation
content: ### DOM Manipulation Once you have selected an element, you can **change** it. --- ### Changing Content ```js const el = document.querySelector("#myDiv"); el.textContent = "New text"; // Sets text only (faster, secure) el.innerHTML = "Bold text"; // Sets HTML (parses tags) el.innerText = "Visible text"; // Respects CSS visibility ``` > **Security:** Use `textContent` (not `innerHTML`) for user-provided content to prevent XSS attacks. --- ### Changing Attributes ```js const img = document.querySelector("img"); img.src = "new-image.jpg"; img.alt = "New description"; img.setAttribute("title", "Tooltip text"); console.log(img.getAttribute("src")); img.removeAttribute("disabled"); ``` --- ### Changing Styles ```js const el = document.querySelector(".box"); el.style.color = "red"; el.style.backgroundColor = "black"; // camelCase, not background-color el.style.fontSize = "20px"; el.style.display = "none"; // Hide element ``` --- ### Working with Classes ```js const el = document.querySelector(".box"); el.classList.add("active"); // Add class el.classList.remove("hidden"); // Remove class el.classList.toggle("visible"); // Toggle on/off el.classList.contains("active"); // Check if has class ``` --- ### Creating and Removing Elements ```js const newDiv = document.createElement("div"); newDiv.textContent = "Hello"; document.body.appendChild(newDiv); // Add to end of body const parent = document.querySelector(".container"); parent.insertBefore(newDiv, parent.firstChild); // Add at beginning parent.removeChild(oldElement); // Remove child el.remove(); // Remove self (modern) ``` --- > **Performance Tip:** Batch DOM changes together. Each DOM manipulation can trigger a reflow/render. Use `document.createDocumentFragment()` for many insertions.

quiz.question: Which property should you use to safely set text content?
